attempt: Strengthen the local obstruction test by measuring how many ordered unit-residue prime pairs cover each even residue class. A local counterexample route would need uncovered or extremely thin classes.
obstruction: The tested primorial residue systems have no uncovered even target classes. Goldbach remains an analytic lower-bound problem, not a local congruence obstruction in these systems.
candidate_theorem: Local multiplicity plus explicit major/minor arc estimates gives a positive lower bound for every sufficiently large even integer, with the remaining small range covered by certificate.
next_experiment: Attach explicit analytic constants to the strongest local multiplicity rows.
claim_boundary: No Goldbach proof and no Goldbach counterexample found.
